description: Despite its minor drawbacks, Acronis Cyber Backup has been a reliable backup solution for our IT services company. Its user-friendly interface, variety of backup options, and fast data recovery make it a valuable tool to have. It does have its share of flaws, like being a bit slow during backups and a somewhat steep price, but in the end, the pros outweigh the cons. If you're looking for a reliable backup solution, I'd definitely recommend giving Acronis Cyber Backup a try!
author: Kavindu Githsara K.
pros: I've been using Acronis Cyber Backup for a while now in our IT services company, and I've got to say, it's a pretty solid backup solution. The best part is its ease of use. I found the interface quite user-friendly, and it didn't take me long to figure out how to create and manage backups. The software also offers a variety of backup options, like full, incremental, and differential backups, which is awesome because it allows me to choose the one that fits my needs the best. Another great feature is the ability to restore data quickly. In the few instances where I've had to restore data, the process was fast and hassle-free.
cons: While Acronis Cyber Backup is a good software, it does have a few drawbacks. One thing I noticed is that it can be a bit slow when performing a backup, especially if you have a lot of data to back up. This can be a bit annoying when you're in a hurry. Also, the software can be a bit pricey, especially if you're a small business or just getting started in the IT industry. I also wish they provided more detailed tutorials or help articles for some of the more advanced features. Sometimes it takes a bit of trial and error to figure things out.

description: If you do not need support, Acronis works very well and has good logging. Any errors can turn into a larger struggle.
author: Caleb S.
pros: Acronis makes it easy to backup locally and remotely and monitor the progress. We can rest assured that the data provided is accurate and setup alerts to better aid us in knowing when something is not functioning properly. It also allows us backup customers O365 data, which is critical. We've found the O365 backups to be the most reliable as they connect directly to MS servers and relieve the painpoints we experience with their agents on the computers.
cons: It often times breaks, especially when an update is pending for the Acronis software. When we need support, they often do not provide good data or take weeks to come up with any solutions. While Mac products have become more stable over the past year, they seem to struggle much more than Windows devices.

review_title: Do NOT give them your credit card info!
description: The absolute worst customer service ever. I was previously using Acronis for personal and business PCs under different subscriptions. I cancelled my personal account year's ago yet their distribution partner, Cleverbridge, renews it every year with credit card info they kept without my permission and multiple advisories to remove same. This year they did it again and it took weeks to get a refund. I have dozens of emails and ridiculous comments from Acronis and Cleverbridge who pass the buck between each other. I never got all my money back and am stuck with them for a few more months. Rest assured I will not renew with them nor will I ever buy software via Cleverbridge. They are in no way GDPR compliant and if they ever have a data breach, know that your credit card info is kept by them regardless of what you allow/tell them. It one thing to create software that works (most of the time) but screwing up customer service like this will hopefully lead them to wake up and treat customers fairly or go out of business...... Happy to share the litany of e-mails that range between ridiculous and humorous. Buyer beware!
author: Rob C.
pros: It worked on Mac and PC which was a plus. This is a product that has evolved over the years so it is still maturing.
cons: When there is a tech issue, their outsourced customer service barely speaks English and it takes a ridiculous amount of messaging to solve the simplest things.

review_title: All in one backup solution which can be opted for without the concern of CAPEX.
description: I am using Acronis for the last could of months and yet I did not face any issue in terms of working as well as their support's response time is also too good.
author: Mukul N.
pros: There is no need for any minimum billing commitment for Aronis cyber cloud (if you opt for the same from Acronis resellers), as well as they offers the backups for each and every type of computing load and Operating Systemes in addition we can monitor and configure the backup from a single pan of glass (i.e. Acronis Console).
cons: We can not configure or use our own AWS S3 or Microsoft Blob as storage.

review_title: The Disaster recovery for your infrastructure
description: Overall Acronis has been rock solid for me. I was surprised by the simplicity at first but have come to embrace it. I had setup complicated Veeam Cloud Connect solutions in previous roles, so in my mind Backup Solution= Complicated. Not so with Acronis. I have had to engage support once or twice, and I'm happy to say they got me what I needed without much fuss.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Initially we needed a backup solution for Office 365 (email,sharepoint,teams etc) and sent out requests to our VAR. He came back with Acronis, which I'd used many years ago personally. Coming from a Veaam background professionally, I was very much going to go with an onsite solution backed by Veeam, but I gave Acronis a try. The Fact that I had everything setup in 10 mins and backing up, kinda sealed the deal. I'd not expected it to be that simple. Later I'd a need to backup a few VM and servers that had no local storage for... no problem, install the agent and it's backed up to Azure Blobs. Also very cool, is that I used Acronis to migrate these servers to Azure eventually, simply be restoring from Acronis to Azure.
cons: Backing up O365 services to Azure blobs? And only to Europe North? Not what I would call Disaster recovery, in the event that Europe North has issues.

review_title: Easy to setup and reliable to restore from.
description: We have been using Acronis for sometime now, and it is a very user friendly solution. It offers lots of options, and the time where we have had to restore, it has been successful every time.
author: Warren L.
pros: One of the best parts of Acronis ,is just how easy it is to setup and configure. It comes with so many options and features. If you want a simple backup, a few clicks and your away. If you want encryption and compression etc. equally a easy,.
cons: What we would like to see, is a way of customizing the email address which sends us the backup alerts. This way we can administer it easier, as it creates tickets in our ticket system.

review_title: Great Backup, When You Know How!
description: I require backup software that is consistently reliable, yet offers the flexibility to backup my entire computer system, including system files. Then, I want to easily restore any of my backup file versions as required, especially if I purchase new computers that need the same configuration as my existing ones. Crucially, if any of my computers should break beyond repair, I want to use my backup files to restore on any new computers. Acronis, delivers on the above. I am very appreciative for this and have had cause to use these features. I am accustomed to a backup routine and do this weekly. I keep three generations of backup. Acronis has a great feature of validating each backup that, I presume checks each file for errors. Comforting to know when your data is important.
author: Francis H.
pros: Acronis has an extensive suite of backup features, with a myriad of options. You will find all that you need from backing up all of your hard drive to selected files. You can backup to an external drive or to the cloud, you decide. Your backup files are available whenever you require, for the inevitable disaster, when you will be thankful you backed up regularly. Its one of the few backup software that actually backs up your entire computer system. Very useful if you have a complete breakdown or need to restore to a new computer.
cons: At first glance, the software can appear daunting to the novice. The Menu looks clean and simple, yet you look around, bemused, because you don't really know what to do. The instructions are not good. I found my way around, more, trial and error. Discovering the features, by exploring the Menu options and reading the prompts. Initially, it took over hours of my time. Now I know what I am doing, I think the software is great. However, if I go on holiday and come back to Acronis after a period of time, it takes a little time to refresh myself on how use it again. Its not intuitive. With this in mind, this is my backup software of choice.

description: Good product but must improve virtual backup
author: Fernando S.
pros: Can backup phisical servers and virtual server quickly
cons: Sometimes, backup works has block my servers and support team never discover why

author: Aaron G.
pros: We use this for workstation backups at my company and for this task it works great. The compression and deduplication is pretty good, scheduling options are very robust. I've had very few issues using it and it has been very reliable.
cons: Compression is pretty good, but not the best out there

review_title: A combination of practicality and userbility
description: The software has made it easy for us to monitor our devices for viruses and malware attacks. It has also provided us with as evidence remote patch management systems.
author: Claudio D.
pros: Fast customer services and ability of the software to effortlessly scan locate endpoints.
cons: Steep learning curve is the only issue with the software.

review_title: My Experience With Acronis Cyber Backup
author: Lucia M.
pros: I like that this is a scalable and an affordable business data backup software. It is very secure and guarantees business data security. Searching and retrieving/recovering backed up data is easy.
cons: With Acronis Cyber Backup, there hasn't been challenges. It is all perfect.

review_title: Perfect All-in-One Backup Platform for any system or cloud service you could think of.
description: Trying to backup various systems (Windows, Android, iOS, Mac) and cloud services (Google Workspace, Microsoft 365) in one platform was our goal. Acronis helped us archive this with ease and a very intuitive interface. All previous support requests we had have been answered and solved within 1-3 hours, if something could not be solved it was handed over to engineering to see if it's possible to implement the desired functionality. Pricing is very fair. You can add additional backup storages either local or at various service providers of your choice like AWS, Google, Azure, etc.
author: Benjamin S.
pros: It is very easy to create and manage various backup plans for different systems, cloud services and use cases. You can easily integrate Google Workspace and Microsoft365 into the platform and have a tree view of all your shared folders and email accounts. Backing up one of these is as easy as clicking a checkbox (if you already setup and defined your backup plan). All backups are by default encrypted, you can however encrypt these again. You can manage disaster recovery and have your backup stored at various service provider of your choice and/or local. Pricing per GB model is very fair and inexpensive.
cons: Might be sometimes overwhelming, as it also tries to integrate some sort of cloud protection option, however easy to ignore if you only want to use it for backups. The interface is not very mobile-friendly, highly recommend using it on PC only.

review_title: Good product but room for improvement of the customer service
description: The software stores back-ups in different locations and does this quite well. Customer support could be much more efficiënt.
author: Rob W.
pros: The software is easy to use, versatile and has many useful options. I run back-ups to an external hard drive and a NAS, also I use it for protection against ransomware. It works quite well in the background and gives me the option to restore files and more when there is a problem. Also, I like the option to be able to restore an image to different hardware
cons: Customer support could be better. Recently I send in a question about an error. I got the request to send in a log file and screenshots, and then a couple of days later, I receive the solution to install the latest update. Which, of course, I already had. After I informed the same person of having previously installed the most recent update, then he sent me the request to send log files and screenshots again. That is one example, but my experience is that the support often works like this. Also, they can ask me for information, schedule a remote control session (which in itself is quite nice), and do exactly what we already discussed themselves. They are but not always very efficiënt.

review_title: I'm a big fan of Acronis for business backup. Very efficient in bringing back up images of servers.
description: Like I said in my review, the virtual image backups are really easy to work with once set up correctly, and can definitely save you from headaches when a server blows up on you. You basically just point to your image, and spin it back up when disaster happens.
author: Chris J.
pros: Our Acronis backup scheme works very well in protecting our main file server. The backup process is quick and offers a lot of options, across a number of different backup devices. It's also very easy to spin up another image of a downed server in a short amount of time. It has saved me when one of our VM's was corrupted, and it was very easy to retrieve files from archived images.
cons: The set up can be a little daunting, especially when working with virtual server technology. Also, there customer support site can be a little overwhelming to wade through for the uninitiated. You have to really identify your issue before sifting through all the support articles. The online manuals were pretty straightforward but did require some study before getting started.

author: Roman M.
pros: The best thing about it is the different options for restoring while keeping RPOs and RTOs low.
cons: The price and that it is not so modular, there are many functionalities that we don't need because we cover them with another product and we can't stop paying for them.

description: Very good , you buy the software , you attach an external drive , you select the drive or folders to be backup and the size of part files and you start it
author: Frederic H.
pros: You can do many types of backup like incremential backup and split it in many files to make it easier to transfer it
cons: No support to backup on the same drive or create an disk image like iso or a virtual machine for vmware or virtualbox

description: Outstanding Product, the very best, a bit expensive but worth the investment!
author: Richard P.
pros: This software has ALL of the features you could ever want in a backup and security system. Scheduling Incremental, Full backup, and Differential Backup and maintaining removal of old backups so space is always available. Bare metal restores from full and Incremental backups. Has wake on Lan so the PC can sleep.
cons: Complicated to install, but no brainer for workstation user once set up.

description: Overall, this is one of the best backup programs I have used. I have had to do several bare metals restores using it and it has always performed perfectly.
author: Gary W.
pros: Acronis backup is something I have been using for years. I switched to it after using another major Vendor's backup solution after it became very costly. Acronis has only become better over the years. Their agents deploy nicely to servers, the workstation backups are flexible, and they have a Mac client.
cons: The only downside I see is that their cost for their online backup is a bit high. If you catch a sale or discount then it isn't too bad.

author: Jorge L.
pros: We have implemented Acronis backup tools in many customers with the purpose of automating workstation and server backup processes. Acronis is consistent in the scheduled tasks and provides a reliable backup (and recovery) solution for organizations of different sizes. On a personal note, I have also used the Acronis Ransomware Protection Tool which is free and a great resource for protecting against the loss of important files. Their software tools, in general, are easy to use with a friendly and intuitive user interface.
cons: Setting up the business tools takes some time and requires attention to detail. You can easily misconfigure important tasks if you are not careful.

review_title: Overall solid and full featured. Can be confusing for less savvy end users
description: A reliable backup software that can be precisely configured as needed
author: Paul K.
pros: Acronis has been my personal backup software of choice for my own office for several years, it has a huge degree of configurability, could look for in a backup product. I find that it does though, require some degree of technical awareness to configure and utilize properly
cons: Interface can be intimidating and /or confusing, for less technically proficient and users. Can present them with too many options and choices.

description: We have had to use the restore feature a few times. On one occasion the backup did not complete and we lost a weeks worth of work when we restored the files. I periodically do a verification to make sure the backup is running correctly. Otherwise its a must have when your computer crashes.
author: Sue C.
pros: Can be set up for daily backups Runs in the background Easy computer restore feature
cons: Some 3red party software has to be reinstalled If for some reason the backup does not complete your backs from that day forward are not usable

author: Michael R.
pros: I really like the software as far as dependability and reliability. I've used this software on multiple platforms for over a decade, one of the most respected names in my mind when it comes to backup/recovery solution providers.
cons: The cost of the server platform and the feature set it offers for that price point is really expensive. But compared to other providers like Veeam it does a much better job and is dependable.

description: I thought it did the job for the most part. I would not say it's the best software out there as I have moved on from Acronis to others. Pricing is a bit high to be honest.
author: Victor A.
pros: The layout of the interface was decent. I was able to customize it to use it for our company with branding. It did give notifications of failed backups on a regular. I was able to easily see what was backed up and when it was backed up.
cons: I thought the pricing is a bit high. I am not sure if it was because I went through a vendor to purchase and deploy this but there were quite a bit of limitations of what I could do as an admin and user. For instance, I was not able to have 2 admins share the same interface and get notifications. Only one of the admins were able to get notifications. Again, I'm not sure if this was because our vendor did not set it up right.

description: I love using Acronis to backup my desktop to my NAS and also to create images of computers that I am working on.
author: Kevin R.
pros: I like how easy and quick it is to make an image of a computer. I also REALLY like the universal restore feature, this is great for restoring a system on dissimilar hardware.
cons: The one con I can think of is that in order to explore an image you have created, you must have the Acronis software installed on your computer to do so. Windows cannot open a .tib file on its own.

author: Syed S.
pros: Ease of usage, performance and reliability
cons: Interface struck sometimes slow down access
